name: Wellspring Rod
sigNote: SIGNATURE "Spiritual Font" (runtime-written restore, SpiritualFont.cs) — REWORKED 2026-06-10: the movement-bit grant is RETIRED (live test answered the open question: both Lifefont 237 + Manafont 238 bits HELD perfectly on +0x9C, but the engine honors exactly ONE movement passive and picked Lifefont — only HP ticked on move). Now the runtime does the font work itself: at each wielder completed-turn edge (the wielder's OWN scheduler CT pull-down -- CtTurns, band +0x25; NOT the acted-edge TurnTracker, whose cursor-following attribution mis-credited turns live), if their gx/gy changed since their previous turn edge (first edge of a battle only snapshots, never fires), write HP += round(maxHP*10%) (floor 1, clamp, NEVER revive) and MP += round(maxMP*10%) (floor 1, clamp; never into a corpse -- a wielder dead at the edge gains nothing). MP rides the PROVISIONAL band +0x18/+0x1A pair — every MP write is gated behind a per-battle layout validation (>= 2 valid band units, all mp <= maxMp, all maxMp <= 999, at least one maxMp >= 1; fail = HP-only that battle) + a post-write SET/MISS read-back. p3Desc: 'Moving restores the wielder's HP and MP.' Knobs: Tuning.FontHpPct/FontMpPct = 0.10. Completes the never-run-dry fantasy: Absorb-MP refuels by poking, the font by walking.

name: Spark Rod

name: Hushward Rod
sigNote: SIGNATURE "Unbroken Chant" (support grant, Signatures/GrowthEngine HoldSignature path) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3 the wielder gains Swiftspell (support 226, +0x98 byte 3 mask 0x08), OR-set + held each tick; stacks with the player's own support pick; released (AND-cleared) if the rod leaves the wielder's hands mid-battle, never stripping a player-picked Swiftspell. Halved spell charge time deepens the anti-Silence identity. p3Desc: 'Grants Swiftspell: the wielder casts in half the usual time.' OPEN LIVE QUESTION: Swiftspell as a LIVE-held bit is unverified — charge time is computed at cast-queue so it should work (it is NOT in the IsBuildTimeOnly set); the GRANT read-back log + one timed cast settles it. Replaces the DRAFT triple stack (invalid multi-grant style).

name: Umbral Rod
sigNote: SIGNATURE "Life Sap" (customRuntime, LifeSap.cs) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3, a kill credited to this rod restores the wielder by 25% of max HP (Tuning.LifeSapPct=0.25; clamped at full; NEVER revives a 0-HP wielder). Trigger = the per-weapon kill-tally diff (the ExtraTurn freshKill pattern), so attribution rides the proven KillTracker credit. Wielder located via roster resolve + band twin-filter walk (Wielder.cs, shared locator split from ExtraTurn). Heal lands on the authoritative band entry HP, Mem.Writable-guarded. p3Desc: 'Defeating a foe restores a quarter of the wielder's health.' Replaces the DRAFT triple support stack (invalid multi-grant style); the dark rod drinks life — on theme with the Absorb-MP sibling.

name: Dragon Rod
sigNote: SIGNATURE "Wyrmblood" (customRuntime, Wyrmblood.cs) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3, at each wielder COMPLETED-turn edge (TurnTracker rising-Acted: the heal lands as the wielder finishes acting, adjacency from the post-move tile — not pre-move like vanilla Regen), the wielder AND every ALLY within 1 Manhattan tile heal their OWN maxHP/8 (Tuning.WyrmbloodDiv = vanilla Regen rate), clamped at full. EMULATED regen — the Regen status bit is unmapped, never touched; guarded HP writes on the authoritative band entries. Allies positively identified (static-array player-slot fingerprints — never enemies); dead never healed; one heal per fingerprint per splash. Each splash dev-logged. p3Desc: 'The wielder regenerates each turn, and the warmth mends adjacent allies.' Name 'Dragonheart' FORBIDDEN (Dragoon reaction collision). Replaces the DRAFT Regenerate stack with the same fantasy in buildable form.

name: Rod of Faith
sigNote: SIGNATURE "Rapture" (customRuntime, Rapture.cs) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3, when the wielder's HP drops below 30% of max (Tuning.RaptureHpPct), Master Teleportation (movement 243 = ability.en key 499; Tuning.RaptureMoveId) REPLACES the movement field (band +0x80, 3 bytes) for 3 completed wielder turns (Tuning.RaptureTurns, TurnTracker), then the saved movement restores — Maim's save-once/hold/restore discipline (never-re-save). Re-arm hysteresis: HP must recover to/above the threshold between windows. Restores on unequip/tier loss/wielder death (3-tick streak)/battle exit; ConditionMet-style integer HP gate. DUAL-WIELD: the held image merges the other hand's earned movement-bit grants (Wellspring fonts) so the two holds never fight over the shared field. CUT-CONTENT CAVEAT: 243 is marked cut in FOLDABLE_ABILITIES — arm-time read-back (SET/MISS) dev-logged once per window settles whether the engine honors it; if MISS, flip Tuning.RaptureMoveId to plain Teleport 242 (one line). p3Desc: 'Below a third health, the wielder gains Master Teleport for three turns.' The glass cannon's escape hatch — divine ecstasy at death's door. Replaces the DRAFT First Strike stack (invalid: reaction slot).
